nonBlockingIssues.description: draw_coverage() assumes opaque destination (correct for framebuffer but undocumented assumption)
blend_over() intermediate product ~4.24B is close to u32::MAX (34M headroom). Safe but worth a comment.
PREV_CURSOR_Y is still written but never read — dead store. Harmless but confusing.
Stale comments: rasterizer.rs header and GlyphCache::zeroed() doc still reference old dimensions.
Downsample uses floor division instead of rounded division (negligible ~0.2% perceptual impact).
No bounds check on buffer_index from MSG_PRESENT payload in GPU driver. Trusted path, low risk.
FB_PTRS is a function-local static mut — inconsistent with other module-level statics.
MAX_DIRTY_RECTS=7 but payload fits only 6 rects. 7th rect silently dropped. Harmless with current 2-rect usage.
DirtyRect/PresentPayload structs duplicated between compositor and GPU driver with manual sync.
Stale docstring in page_allocator.rs says '4 MiB' but MAX_ORDER is now 11 (8 MiB).
No upper-bound validation on queried display dimensions (graceful exit on alloc failure exists).
Redundant mmio_pa/irq fields in phase-4 GpuConfig message (GPU driver already has them).

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Rendering Invariants' section to architecture.md: clear-before-re-render rule and gamma-correct blending documentation
Added 'DMA Allocation Limits' section to architecture.md: MAX_DMA_ORDER constraint and two-alloc workaround pattern
Updated Font Pipeline section in architecture.md: now documents 2x horizontal + 4x vertical oversampling, gamma-correct blending, updated GlyphCache size estimate

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update test baseline count from '607+ tests' to '719+ tests' (698 system + 21 prototype as of crisp-and-smooth completion). The current number could cause workers to think they've introduced a regression when adding tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: AGENTS.md says '607+ tests must pass at all times (586 system + 21 prototype)'. Actual count after all crisp-and-smooth features: 719. Feature 2d-oversampling reviewer flagged this discrepancy.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance: 'IPC payload budget is 60 bytes TOTAL including header fields. When designing message structs, account for type discriminants, counts, and padding within the 60 bytes — not just the array data.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: damage-tracking feature description said 'up to 7 rects per message' based on 60/8=7, but the actual payload has 8 bytes of header (buffer_index + rect_count), leaving room for only 6 rects. Worker set MAX_DIRTY_RECTS=7 matching the spec rather than the reality.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: os-worker skill should add to its constraints section: 'Userspace stack is 16 KiB. All userspace code is compiled with -C opt-level=s. Avoid large stack allocations, deep recursion, or inline(never) on hot paths. If you see mysterious crashes in userspace, check stack overflow first.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: gamma-correct-blending worker discovered stack overflow from unoptimized code (lookup tables on stack). Fixed via build.rs opt-level=s. The constraint is in architecture.md but not in the skill workers actually read first.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When changing kernel or library constants (MAX_ORDER, MAX_DMA_ORDER, etc.), search for ALL comments and docstrings that reference the old value. Module-level docstrings, inline comments, and log strings all need updating.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: configurable-resolution changed MAX_ORDER from 10 to 11 but missed the module docstring in page_allocator.rs that says '4 MiB'. Worker updated the constant and its adjacent comment but not the distant docstring.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md should mention opt-level=s constraint (from gamma-correct-blending review)
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in .factory/library/architecture.md 'Stack Constraint' section. AGENTS.md doesn't need to duplicate this; the skill suggestion above covers the worker-facing gap.

rejectedObservations.observation: os-worker skill should mention handle_send constraint on unstarted processes (from configurable-resolution review)
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in .factory/library/architecture.md 'Process Startup Constraint' section. Library is the right place for factual codebase knowledge.

rejectedObservations.observation: IPC payload structs duplicated between compositor and GPU driver (from damage-tracking review)
rejectedObservations.reason: Known architectural limitation — processes are separate bare-metal binaries that can't share libraries easily. The 'must match' comments are the current mitigation. A shared protocol crate would be an architecture change beyond a convention fix.
